rationale: A better matches the actual Kafka Connect 4.2.0 base contract: `Connector` Javadoc plus direct `connect/api` unit tests (`ConnectorTest`, `SourceConnectorTest`, `SinkConnectorTest`) are the right anchors. B has good version-pinned docs, but it misses the direct api-module tests and instead leans on runtime integration tests that are adjacent rather than contract-defining.

A: Does not give the exact 4.2.0 Javadoc URL, only the class/name and artifact.
Could be slightly more explicit that `taskClass` and `taskConfigs` are base-`Connector` methods, not just generic lifecycle hooks.
B: Claims the retrieval system did not surface a dedicated `Connector` unit test suite, but `connect/api/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/connector/ConnectorTest.java` exists in 4.2.0.
Leans on `ConnectorValidationIntegrationTest` and `ExactlyOnceSourceIntegrationTest`, which are useful runtime neighbors but not the closest anchors for the base connector contract.
